ddls:
CREATE TABLE "system_metrics" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system metric */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., System, Application) */,
  "signal_index" INTEGER /* Signal index of the metric */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., CPU Usage, Memory Usage) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "assembly_name" TEXT /* Assembly name of the metric */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Type name of the metric */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Method name of the metric */,
  "arguments" TEXT /* Arguments for the metric */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Whether the metric is enabled */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the metric (e.g., System.Double) */,
  "display_format" TEXT /* Display format of the metric (e.g., {0:N3}) */,
  "is_connected_state" INTEGER /* Whether the metric is connected to a state */,
  "load_order" INTEGER /* Load order of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., %, MB, ms) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the metric is collected (e.g., every 5 minutes) */,
  "last_collected_time" TEXT /* The last time the metric was collected */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Default threshold value for the metric (e.g., 80% for CPU usage) */,
  "alert_on_exceed" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if an alert should be triggered when the metric exceeds the threshold */,
  "alert_on_fall_below" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if an alert should be triggered when the metric falls below the threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_display_format FOREIGN KEY ("display_format") REFERENCES display_formats ("name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES data_sources ("name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_metrics_data_type FOREIGN KEY ("data_type") REFERENCES metric_types ("name")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., System, Application) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., system, application, custom) */,
  "connection_string" TEXT /* Connection string or configuration details for the data source */,
  "last_sync_time" TEXT /* The last time the data source was synchronized */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metric_types" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric type */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the metric type (e.g., System.Double) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric type */,
  "min_value" REAL /* The minimum value that the metric can have */,
  "max_value" REAL /* The maximum value that the metric can have */,
  "default_value" REAL /* The default value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "display_formats" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each display format */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the display format (e.g., {0:N3}) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the display format */,
  "color" TEXT /* The color to be used for displaying the metric on the dashboard */,
  "icon" TEXT /* The icon to be used for displaying the metric on the dashboard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Administrator, User) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the dashboard */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the dashboard was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the dashboard was last updated */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if this is the default dashboard for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboards_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboard_widgets" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each widget */,
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dashboard that the widget belongs to */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric displayed on the widget */,
  "x" INTEGER /* X-coordinate of the widget on the dashboard */,
  "y" INTEGER /* Y-coordinate of the widget on the dashboard */,
  "width" INTEGER /* Width of the widget */,
  "height" INTEGER /* Height of the widget */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the widget */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the widget was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the widget was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboard_widgets_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES system_metrics ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboard_widgets_dashboard_id FOREIGN KEY ("dashboard_id") REFERENCES dashboards ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_rules" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert rule */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that the rule applies to */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the rule */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Operator for the rule (e.g., greater than, less than) */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method of notification for the rule (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the alert rule was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* The date and time when the alert rule was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the alert rule is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_rules_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES system_metrics ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the notification */,
  "alert_rule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert rule that triggered the notification */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Time when the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* The message content of the notification */,
  "status" TEXT /* The status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_rule_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_rule_id") REFERENCES alert_rules ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metric_values" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric value */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that the value belongs to */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time when the value was recorded */,
  "collected_by" TEXT /* The user or system that collected the metric value */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* The IP address of the source where the metric value was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_values_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES system_metrics ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_events" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of the event (e.g., system crash, error) */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Time when the event occurred */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "severity" TEXT /* The severity level of the event (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* The IP address where the event occurred */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean flag to indicate if the event has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* The user who resolved the event */,
  "resolved_time" TEXT /* The date and time when the event was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
